inputs.label: Project Type
inputs.businessContext: Determines base labor factors per project complexity. Based on industry standards (NECA Manual of Labor Units).

inputs.label: Total Circuit Length
inputs.businessContext: Total length of electrical circuits (conduit, cable tray, etc.). Affects pulling, termination, and testing labor.

inputs.label: Number of Devices
inputs.businessContext: Count of outlets, switches, fixtures, panels, etc. Each device adds labor for mounting and wiring.

inputs.label: Labor Rate
inputs.businessContext: Hourly wage including burden. Based on local union rates or market surveys (RSMeans).

inputs.label: Efficiency Factor
inputs.businessContext: Adjusts for crew productivity (1.0 = standard). Factors: experience, tools, site conditions. Lean principles.

inputs.label: Complexity Factor
inputs.businessContext: Multiplier for difficult installations (cramped spaces, high ceilings, etc.). Based on NECA labor units.

inputs.label: Data Confidence
inputs.businessContext: Confidence in input data accuracy (1.0 = high). Adjusts final estimate for risk. ISO 9001 data quality.

rules: If projectType is 'emergency-repair', then laborRate must be >= 1.5 * standard laborRate (minimum 60).
If projectType is 'maintenance', then totalCircuitLength must be > 0.

thresholds.efficiencyFactor < 0.7: Low efficiency may indicate training or tool issues.
thresholds.complexityFactor > 1.5: High complexity may require specialized crew.
thresholds.dataConfidence < 0.7: Low data confidence; consider sensitivity analysis.

suggestedActions: If efficiencyFactor < 0.7: Provide training or improve tools.
If complexityFactor > 1.5: Consider pre-fabrication or specialized crew.
If dataConfidence < 0.7: Verify input data with site walkdown.
